summary: The thoracic duct drains the entire body except the right upper quadrant (right head, neck, and right upper limb).
The thoracic duct drains only the lower limbs and abdomen.
The right lymphatic duct drains the entire body except the left upper limb.
Approximately 3 litres of interstitial fluid per day is returned to the blood via the lymphatic system.
Approximately 10 litres of fluid leaks into the interstitium daily.
The lymphatic system returns about 500 mL of fluid per day.
Lacteals are specialised lymphatic capillaries in intestinal villi that absorb dietary fats as chylomicrons.
Lacteals are blood capillaries that absorb amino acids from the gut.
Lacteals are found in the stomach and absorb bile salts.
Lymphatic capillaries have a discontinuous basement membrane and overlapping endothelial cells.
Lymphatic capillaries have a continuous basement membrane with tight junctions.
Lymphatic capillaries are identical in structure to blood capillaries.
Secondary lymphoedema in coastal Tamil Nadu is commonly caused by Wuchereria bancrofti (filariasis).
Secondary lymphoedema in Tamil Nadu is caused by tuberculosis of lymph nodes.
Secondary lymphoedema is always a congenital condition.
The thoracic duct begins as the cisterna chyli and drains into the left subclavian-internal jugular junction.
The thoracic duct begins at the thoracic inlet and drains into the right atrium.
The cisterna chyli is located in the thorax at the level of T12.
